001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.collections;
018
019import static java.util.Collections.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
023
024import java.lang.reflect.*;
025import java.util.*;
026import java.util.function.*;
027
028/**
029 * A fluent builder for constructing {@link Set} instances with various configuration options.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * This builder provides a flexible and type-safe way to construct sets with support for adding elements,
033 * collections, arrays, sorting, and applying modifiers like unmodifiable or sparse modes. Sets automatically
034 * handle duplicates - adding the same element multiple times will result in only one occurrence in the final set.
035 *
036 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
037 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
038 *    <li>Fluent API - all methods return <c>this</c> for method chaining
039 *    <li>Multiple add methods - single elements, varargs, collections, arrays
040 *    <li>Arbitrary input support - automatic type conversion with {@link #addAny(Object...)}
041 *    <li>Conditional adding - {@link #addIf(boolean, Object)} for conditional elements
042 *    <li>Sorting support - natural order or custom {@link Comparator}
043 *    <li>Sparse mode - return <jk>null</jk> for empty sets
044 *    <li>Unmodifiable mode - create immutable sets
045 *    <li>Filtering support - exclude unwanted elements via {@link #filtered()} or {@link #filtered(Predicate)}
046 *    <li>Custom conversion functions - type conversion via {@link #elementFunction(Function)}
047 *    <li>Automatic deduplication - duplicate elements are automatically removed
048 * </ul>
049 *
050 * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
051 * <p class='bjava'>
052 *    <jc>// Basic usage</jc>
053 *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>set</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
054 *       .add(<js>"apple"</js>, <js>"banana"</js>, <js>"cherry"</js>)
055 *       .build();
056 *
057 *    <jc>// Automatic deduplication</jc>
058 *    Set&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>unique</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
059 *       .add(1, 2, 3, 2, 1)  <jc>// Duplicates ignored</jc>
060 *       .build();  <jc>// Contains: 1, 2, 3</jc>
061 *
062 *    <jc>// With sorting</jc>
063 *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>sorted</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
064 *       .add(<js>"zebra"</js>, <js>"apple"</js>, <js>"banana"</js>)
065 *       .sorted()
066 *       .build();  <jc>// Returns TreeSet in natural order</jc>
067 *
068 *    <jc>// Conditional elements</jc>
069 *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>features</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
070 *       .add(<js>"basic"</js>)
071 *       .addIf(<jv>hasPremium</jv>, <js>"premium"</js>)
072 *       .addIf(<jv>hasEnterprise</jv>, <js>"enterprise"</js>)
073 *       .build();
074 *
075 *    <jc>// Immutable set</jc>
076 *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>immutable</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
077 *       .add(<js>"read"</js>, <js>"only"</js>)
078 *       .unmodifiable()
079 *       .build();
080 *
081 *    <jc>// From multiple sources</jc>
082 *    Set&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>existing</jv> = Set.of(1, 2, 3);
083 *    Set&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>combined</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
084 *       .addAll(<jv>existing</jv>)
085 *       .add(4, 5, 6)
086 *       .build();
087 *
088 *    <jc>// Sparse mode - returns null when empty</jc>
089 *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>maybeNull</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
090 *       .sparse()
091 *       .build();  <jc>// Returns null, not empty set</jc>
092 *
093 *    <jc>// FluentSet wrapper - use buildFluent()</jc>
094 *    FluentSet&lt;String&gt; <jv>fluent</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
095 *       .add(<js>"one"</js>, <js>"two"</js>)
096 *       .buildFluent();
097 *
098 *    <jc>// FilteredSet - use buildFiltered()</jc>
099 *    FilteredSet&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>filtered</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
100 *       .filtered(v -&gt; v &gt; 0)
101 *       .add(5)
102 *       .add(-1)  <jc>// Filtered out</jc>
103 *       .buildFiltered();
104 * </p>
105 *
106 * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
107 * <p>
108 * This class is <b>not thread-safe</b>. Each builder instance should be used by a single thread or
109 * properly synchronized.
110 *
111 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
112 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauCommonsCollections">Collections Package</a>
113 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Lists}
114 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Maps}
115 * </ul>
116 *
117 * @param <E> The element type.
118 */
119@SuppressWarnings({
120   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
121   "java:S3740"  // Raw Set/Iterable types used in utility methods where element type is not statically known
122})
123public class Sets<E> {
124
125   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
126   private static final String ARG_comparator = "comparator";
127   private static final String ARG_elementFunction = "elementFunction";
128   private static final String ARG_elementType = "elementType";
129   private static final String ARG_filter = "filter";
130   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
131   private static final String ARG_values = "values";
132
133   /**
134    * Static creator.
135    *
136    * @param <E> The element type.
137    * @param elementType The element type. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
138    * @return A new builder.
139    */
140   public static <E> Sets<E> create(Class<E> elementType) {
141      return new Sets<>(assertArgNotNull(ARG_elementType, elementType));
142   }
143
144   private Set<E> set;
145   private boolean unmodifiable;
146   private boolean sparse;
147   private boolean concurrent;
148   private boolean ordered = false;
149
150   private Comparator<E> comparator;
151   private Predicate<E> filter;
152   private Class<E> elementType;
153   private Function<Object,E> elementFunction;
154
155   /**
156    * Constructor.
157    *
158    * @param elementType The element type. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
159    */
160   public Sets(Class<E> elementType) {
161      this.elementType = assertArgNotNull(ARG_elementType, elementType);
162   }
163
164   /**
165    * Adds a single value to this set.
166    *
167    * <p>
168    * Note: Filtering is applied at build time, not when adding elements.
169    *
170    * @param value The value to add to this set.
171    * @return This object.
172    */
173   public Sets<E> add(E value) {
174      if (set == null) {
175         if (ordered)
176            set = new LinkedHashSet<>();
177         else if (nn(comparator))
178            set = new TreeSet<>(comparator);
179         else
180            set = new HashSet<>();
181      }
182      set.add(value);
183      return this;
184   }
185
186   /**
187    * Adds multiple values to this set.
188    *
189    * @param values The values to add to this set.
190    * @return This object.
191    */
192   @SuppressWarnings({
193      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for varargs addition
194   })
195   public Sets<E> add(E...values) {
196      assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values);
197      for (var v : values)
198         add(v);
199      return this;
200   }
201
202   /**
203    * Appends the contents of the specified collection into this set.
204    *
205    * <p>
206    * This is a no-op if the value is <jk>null</jk>.
207    *
208    * @param value The collection to add to this set.
209    * @return This object.
210    */
211   public Sets<E> addAll(Collection<E> value) {
212      if (nn(value)) {
213         if (set == null) {
214            if (ordered)
215               set = new LinkedHashSet<>(value);
216            else if (nn(comparator))
217               set = new TreeSet<>(comparator);
218            else
219               set = new HashSet<>(value);
220         } else {
221            set.addAll(value);
222         }
223      }
224      return this;
225   }
226
227   /**
228    * Adds arbitrary values to this set.
229    *
230    * <p>
231    * Objects can be any of the following:
232    * <ul>
233    *    <li>The same type or convertible to the element type of this set.
234    *    <li>Collections or arrays of anything on this set.
235    *    <li>JSON array strings parsed and convertible to the element type of this set.
236    * </ul>
237    *
238    * @param values The values to add.
239    * @return This object.
240    */
241   @SuppressWarnings({
242      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for adding elements of various types
243   })
244   public Sets<E> addAny(Object...values) {
245      if (nn(values)) {
246         for (var o : values) {
247            if (nn(o)) {
248               if (o instanceof Collection<?> o2) {
249                  o2.forEach(this::addAny);
250               } else if (isArray(o)) {
251                  for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(o); i++)
252                     addAny(Array.get(o, i));
253               } else if (elementType.isInstance(o)) {
254                  add(elementType.cast(o));
255               } else {
256                  E converted = convertElement(o);
257                  if (converted != null) {
258                     add(converted);
259                  } else {
260                     throw rex("Object of type {0} could not be converted to type {1}", cn(o), cn(elementType));
261                  }
262               }
263            }
264         }
265      }
266      return this;
267   }
268
269   /**
270    * Adds a value to this set if the specified flag is true.
271    *
272    * @param flag The flag.
273    * @param value The value.
274    * @return This object.
275    */
276   public Sets<E> addIf(boolean flag, E value) {
277      if (flag)
278         add(value);
279      return this;
280   }
281
282   /**
283    * Adds entries to this set via JSON array strings.
284    *
285    * @param values The JSON array strings to parse and add to this set.
286    * @return This object.
287    */
288   public Sets<E> addJson(String...values) {
289      return addAny((Object[])values);
290   }
291
292   /**
293    * Builds the set.
294    *
295    * <p>
296    * Applies filtering, sorting, ordering, concurrent, unmodifiable, and sparse options.
297    *
298    * <p>
299    * Set type selection:
300    * <ul>
301    *    <li>If {@link #sorted()} is set: Uses {@link TreeSet} (or synchronized TreeSet if concurrent)
302    *    <li>If {@link #ordered()} is set: Uses {@link LinkedHashSet} (or synchronized LinkedHashSet if concurrent)
303    *    <li>Otherwise: Uses {@link HashSet} (or synchronized HashSet if concurrent)
304    * </ul>
305    *
306    * <p>
307    * If filtering is applied, the result is wrapped in a {@link FilteredSet}.
308    *
309    * @return The built set, or {@code null} if {@link #sparse()} is set and the set is empty.
310    */
311   @SuppressWarnings({
312      "java:S1168"  // Intentional null when sparse+empty — null is the absent/not-set sentinel used by callers (e.g. OpenAPI bean setters)
313   })
314   public Set<E> build() {
315      if (sparse && ie(set))
316         return null;
317
318      Set<E> set2 = null;
319
320      if (ordered) {
321         set2 = new LinkedHashSet<>();
322      } else if (nn(comparator)) {
323         set2 = new TreeSet<>(comparator);
324      } else {
325         set2 = new HashSet<>();
326      }
327
328      if (concurrent)
329         set2 = synchronizedSet(set2);
330
331      if (nn(filter) || nn(elementFunction)) {
332         var set3b = FilteredSet.create(elementType);
333         if (nn(filter))
334            set3b.filter(filter);
335         if (nn(elementFunction))
336            set3b.elementFunction(elementFunction);
337         set2 = set3b.inner(set2).build();
338      }
339
340      if (nn(set))
341         set2.addAll(set);
342
343      if (unmodifiable)
344         set2 = unmodifiableSet(set2);
345
346      return set2;
347   }
348
349   /**
350    * Builds the set and wraps it in a {@link FluentSet}.
351    *
352    * <p>
353    * This is a convenience method that calls {@link #build()} and wraps the result in a {@link FluentSet}.
354    *
355    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
356    * <p class='bjava'>
357    *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
358    *
359    *    FluentSet&lt;String&gt; <jv>set</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
360    *       .add(<js>"one"</js>, <js>"two"</js>)
361    *       .buildFluent();
362    * </p>
363    *
364    * @return The built set wrapped in a {@link FluentSet}, or {@code null} if {@link #sparse()} is set and the set is empty.
365    */
366   public FluentSet<E> buildFluent() {
367      Set<E> result = build();
368      return result == null ? null : new FluentSet<>(result);
369   }
370
371   /**
372    * Builds the set as a {@link FilteredSet}.
373    *
374    * <p>
375    * Set type selection:
376    * <ul>
377    *    <li>If {@link #sorted()} is set: Uses {@link TreeSet} (or synchronized TreeSet if concurrent)
378    *    <li>If {@link #ordered()} is set: Uses {@link LinkedHashSet} (or synchronized LinkedHashSet if concurrent)
379    *    <li>Otherwise: Uses {@link HashSet} (or synchronized HashSet if concurrent)
380    * </ul>
381    *
382    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
383    * <p class='bjava'>
384    *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
385    *
386    *    FilteredSet&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>set</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
387    *       .filtered(v -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
388    *       .add(5)
389    *       .add(-1)  <jc>// Will be filtered out</jc>
390    *       .buildFiltered();
391    * </p>
392    *
393    * <p>
394    * Note: If {@link #unmodifiable()} is set, the returned set will be wrapped in an unmodifiable view,
395    * which may cause issues if the FilteredSet tries to modify it internally. It's recommended to avoid
396    * using {@link #unmodifiable()} when calling this method.
397    *
398    * @return The built set as a {@link FilteredSet}, or {@code null} if {@link #sparse()} is set and the set is empty.
399    */
400   @SuppressWarnings({
401      "java:S1168" // Propagates the documented sparse+empty null from build(); callers rely on null to mean "absent" (tests assert null).
402   })
403   public FilteredSet<E> buildFiltered() {
404      var s = build();
405      if (s == null)  // sparse mode and empty
406         return null;
407      if (s instanceof FilteredSet<E> s2)
408         return s2;
409      // Note that if unmodifiable is true, 's' will be unmodifiable and will cause an error if you try
410      // to insert a value from within FilteredSet.
411      return FilteredSet.create(elementType).inner(s).build();
412   }
413
414   /**
415    * Sets the element conversion function for converting elements in {@link #addAny(Object...)}.
416    *
417    * <p>
418    * The function is applied to each element when adding elements in {@link #addAny(Object...)}.
419    *
420    * @param elementFunction The function to convert elements. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
421    * @return This object.
422    */
423   public Sets<E> elementFunction(Function<Object,E> elementFunction) {
424      this.elementFunction = assertArgNotNull(ARG_elementFunction, elementFunction);
425      return this;
426   }
427
428   /**
429    * Specifies the element type on this list.
430    *
431    * @param value The element type. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
432    * @return This object.
433    */
434   public Sets<E> elementType(Class<E> value) {
435      elementType = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
436      return this;
437   }
438
439   /**
440    * Applies a default filter that excludes common "empty" or "unset" values from being added to the set.
441    *
442    * <p>
443    * The following values are filtered out:
444    * <ul>
445    *    <li>{@code null}
446    *    <li>{@link Boolean#FALSE}
447    *    <li>Numbers with {@code intValue() == -1}
448    *    <li>Empty arrays
449    *    <li>Empty {@link Map Maps}
450    *    <li>Empty {@link Collection Collections}
451    * </ul>
452    *
453    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
454    * <p class='bjava'>
455    *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
456    *
457    *    Set&lt;Object&gt; <jv>set</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(Object.<jk>class</jk>)
458    *       .filtered()
459    *       .add(<js>"name"</js>)
460    *       .add(-1)              <jc>// Filtered out at build time</jc>
461    *       .add(<jk>false</jk>)     <jc>// Filtered out at build time</jc>
462    *       .add(<jk>new</jk> String[0]) <jc>// Filtered out at build time</jc>
463    *       .build();
464    * </p>
465    *
466    * @return This object.
467    */
468   public Sets<E> filtered() {
469      // @formatter:off
470      return filtered(v -> ! (
471         v == null
472         || (v instanceof Boolean v2 && v2.equals(false))
473         || (v instanceof Number v3 && v3.intValue() == -1)
474         || (isArray(v) && Array.getLength(v) == 0)
475         || (v instanceof Map v2 && v2.isEmpty())
476         || (v instanceof Collection v3 && v3.isEmpty())
477         ));
478      // @formatter:on
479   }
480
481   /**
482    * Applies a filter predicate to elements when building the set.
483    *
484    * <p>
485    * The filter receives the element value. Elements where the predicate returns
486    * {@code true} will be kept; elements where it returns {@code false} will be filtered out.
487    *
488    * <p>
489    * This method can be called multiple times. When called multiple times, all filters are combined
490    * using AND logic - an element must pass all filters to be kept in the set.
491    *
492    * <p>
493    * Note: Filtering is applied at build time, not when adding elements.
494    *
495    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
496    * <p class='bjava'>
497    *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
498    *
499    *    <jc>// Keep only non-null, positive integers</jc>
500    *    Set&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>set</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
501    *       .filtered(v -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
502    *       .add(5)
503    *       .add(-1)     <jc>// Filtered out at build time</jc>
504    *       .add(<jk>null</jk>) <jc>// Filtered out at build time</jc>
505    *       .build();
506    *
507    *    <jc>// Multiple filters combined with AND</jc>
508    *    Set&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>set2</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
509    *       .filtered(v -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk>)           <jc>// First filter</jc>
510    *       .filtered(v -&gt; v &gt; 0)                    <jc>// Second filter (ANDed with first)</jc>
511    *       .filtered(v -&gt; v &lt; 100);                  <jc>// Third filter (ANDed with previous)</jc>
512    *       .add(5)
513    *       .add(150)  <jc>// Filtered out (not &lt; 100)</jc>
514    *       .add(-1)   <jc>// Filtered out (not &gt; 0)</jc>
515    *       .build();
516    * </p>
517    *
518    * @param filter The filter predicate. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
519    * @return This object.
520    */
521   public Sets<E> filtered(Predicate<E> filter) {
522      Predicate<E> newFilter = assertArgNotNull(ARG_filter, filter);
523      if (this.filter == null)
524         this.filter = newFilter;
525      else
526         this.filter = this.filter.and(newFilter);
527      return this;
528   }
529
530   /**
531    * Converts the set into a {@link SortedSet}.
532    *
533    * <p>
534    * Note: If {@link #ordered()} was previously called, calling this method will override it.
535    * The last method called ({@link #ordered()} or {@link #sorted()}) determines the final set type.
536    *
537    * @return This object.
538    */
539   @SuppressWarnings({
540      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for sorted set creation
541   })
542   public Sets<E> sorted() {
543      return sorted((Comparator<E>)Comparator.naturalOrder());
544   }
545
546   /**
547    * Converts the set into a {@link SortedSet} using the specified comparator.
548    *
549    * <p>
550    * Note: If {@link #ordered()} was previously called, calling this method will override it.
551    * The last method called ({@link #ordered()} or {@link #sorted()}) determines the final set type.
552    *
553    * @param comparator The comparator to use for sorting. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
554    * @return This object.
555    */
556   public Sets<E> sorted(Comparator<E> comparator) {
557      this.comparator = assertArgNotNull(ARG_comparator, comparator);
558      ordered = false;
559      return this;
560   }
561
562   /**
563    * When specified, the {@link #build()} method will return <jk>null</jk> if the set is empty.
564    *
565    * <p>
566    * Otherwise {@link #build()} will never return <jk>null</jk>.
567    *
568    * @return This object.
569    */
570   public Sets<E> sparse() {
571      sparse = true;
572      return this;
573   }
574
575
576   /**
577    * When specified, {@link #build()} will return an unmodifiable set.
578    *
579    * @return This object.
580    */
581   public Sets<E> unmodifiable() {
582      unmodifiable = true;
583      return this;
584   }
585
586   /**
587    * When specified, {@link #build()} will return a thread-safe synchronized set.
588    *
589    * <p>
590    * The thread-safety implementation depends on other settings:
591    * <ul>
592    *    <li>If {@link #sorted()} is set: Uses synchronized {@link TreeSet}
593    *    <li>If {@link #ordered()} is set: Uses synchronized {@link LinkedHashSet}
594    *    <li>Otherwise: Uses synchronized {@link HashSet}
595    * </ul>
596    *
597    * <p>
598    * This is useful when the set needs to be accessed from multiple threads.
599    *
600    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
601    * <p class='bjava'>
602    *    <jc>// Create a thread-safe set using synchronized HashSet</jc>
603    *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>set</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
604    *       .add(<js>"one"</js>, <js>"two"</js>)
605    *       .concurrent()
606    *       .build();
607    *
608    *    <jc>// Create a thread-safe ordered set</jc>
609    *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>set2</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
610    *       .ordered()
611    *       .concurrent()
612    *       .add(<js>"one"</js>)
613    *       .build();
614    * </p>
615    *
616    * @return This object.
617    */
618   public Sets<E> concurrent() {
619      concurrent = true;
620      return this;
621   }
622
623   /**
624    * Sets whether {@link #build()} should return a thread-safe synchronized set.
625    *
626    * <p>
627    * The thread-safety implementation depends on other settings:
628    * <ul>
629    *    <li>If {@link #sorted()} is set: Uses synchronized {@link TreeSet}
630    *    <li>If {@link #ordered()} is set: Uses synchronized {@link LinkedHashSet}
631    *    <li>Otherwise: Uses synchronized {@link HashSet}
632    * </ul>
633    *
634    * <p>
635    * This is useful when the set needs to be accessed from multiple threads.
636    *
637    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
638    * <p class='bjava'>
639    *    <jc>// Conditionally create a thread-safe set</jc>
640    *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>set</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
641    *       .add(<js>"one"</js>, <js>"two"</js>)
642    *       .concurrent(<jv>needsThreadSafety</jv>)
643    *       .build();
644    * </p>
645    *
646    * @param value Whether to make the set thread-safe.
647    * @return This object.
648    */
649   public Sets<E> concurrent(boolean value) {
650      concurrent = value;
651      return this;
652   }
653
654   /**
655    * When specified, {@link #build()} will use a {@link LinkedHashSet} to preserve insertion order.
656    *
657    * <p>
658    * If not specified, a {@link HashSet} is used by default (no guaranteed order).
659    *
660    * <p>
661    * Note: If {@link #sorted()} was previously called, calling this method will override it.
662    * The last method called ({@link #ordered()} or {@link #sorted()}) determines the final set type.
663    *
664    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
665    * <p class='bjava'>
666    *    <jc>// Create an ordered set (preserves insertion order)</jc>
667    *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>set</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
668    *       .ordered()
669    *       .add(<js>"one"</js>)
670    *       .add(<js>"two"</js>)
671    *       .build();
672    * </p>
673    *
674    * @return This object.
675    */
676   public Sets<E> ordered() {
677      return ordered(true);
678   }
679
680   /**
681    * Sets whether {@link #build()} should use a {@link LinkedHashSet} to preserve insertion order.
682    *
683    * <p>
684    * If <c>false</c> (default), a {@link HashSet} is used (no guaranteed order).
685    * If <c>true</c>, a {@link LinkedHashSet} is used (preserves insertion order).
686    *
687    * <p>
688    * Note: If {@link #sorted()} was previously called, calling this method with <c>true</c> will override it.
689    * The last method called ({@link #ordered()} or {@link #sorted()}) determines the final set type.
690    *
691    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
692    * <p class='bjava'>
693    *    <jc>// Conditionally create an ordered set</jc>
694    *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>set</jv> = Sets.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>)
695    *       .ordered(<jv>preserveOrder</jv>)
696    *       .add(<js>"one"</js>)
697    *       .build();
698    * </p>
699    *
700    * @param value Whether to preserve insertion order.
701    * @return This object.
702    */
703   public Sets<E> ordered(boolean value) {
704      ordered = value;
705      if (ordered)
706         comparator = null;
707      return this;
708   }
709
710   /**
711    * Converts an element object to the element type.
712    *
713    * @param o The object to convert.
714    * @return The converted element, or <jk>null</jk> if conversion is not possible.
715    */
716   @SuppressWarnings({
717      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to E for element conversion
718   })
719   private E convertElement(Object o) {
720      if (elementType.isInstance(o))
721         return (E)o;
722      if (nn(elementFunction))
723         return elementFunction.apply(o);
724      return null;
725   }
726}
